title: [InstCombine] Restrain folding switch condition operation into case to one-use
author: antoniofrighetto
description: It may be profitable to simplify the switch condition operation into their cases if such a condition is used only by the switch.

tests.test_body:
define i1 @test_switch_with_xor_condition_multiple_uses(i32 %x) {
entry:
  %xor = xor i32 %x, 2
  call void @opaque(i32 %xor)
  switch i32 %xor, label %sw.default [
    i32 1, label %sw.bb
    i32 2, label %sw.bb
    i32 3, label %sw.bb
  ]

sw.bb:                                            ; preds = %entry, %entry, %entry
  ret i1 true

sw.default:                                       ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

declare void @opaque(i32)

comments.author: antoniofrighetto
comments.body:
> Makes sense to me since it should not change the density of cases.
> 
> Can you further investigate the diff in bench/clamav/optimized/pdf.ll? The former form is better than the latter one.
> 
> Even if we cannot save one instruction here, I guess it is still profitable if we can make the cases more canonical (e.g, smaller active bits?).

The regression in bench/clamav/optimized/pdf.ll is a bit unfortunate, however it looks like the other user is a PHI node, as a loop-carried value. We could relax the one-use check to allow use of the condition value in PNs too. Not sure if it makes sense fine-tuning this bit at this level of granularity though. Overall, it seems like the improvements outweigh the regressions.
